62080914

I hope you're bouncing up and down on that bottom. I hope you're eating that bottom out and scooping it up. Mmmm, that delicious Vishnu bottom. It has a great taste inside my mouth. 62080937
>>62079649
DOT is literally the most superior blockchain out there. Everything that other people promise and don't deliver DOT already has. Everything that other people lie about and can't do DOT can do. The really BIG upgrade we're waiting on is JAM and NOMT this year
Read more here >>62026407 and >>62056612 and >>62056570. NOMT + JAM scale the network to more a few million or maybe 10-20m TPS. We currently have the highest recorded TPS on a live network (https://data.parity.io/spammening). 143.3k TPS on a live network (NOT TEST NET BULLSHITERY like the other chains are showcasing). This was done one 23 of the 100 cores that are available. So this was only 23% of the full capacity. Some cores are already sold to other chains therefore we can't do a full test. But you can guesstimate the numbers, right? And since the last spammening the throughput has gone up. We could now probably do like 250k TPS from 23 cores or something like that. Just look at the "Performance Charts" on the page I linked. The fees were literally almost always below 0.05$. The fees were around 0.03 to 0.015$ even when the network handled 140k TPS. Do you remember how Solana halted the network literally 8x due to it being congested and shit like that? That can't happen on DOT. The average finality was 16.58s. The thing is though, we'll soon be getting 500ms blocks. When the tests were conducted the block times were still 12s? Now they're 6s and soon they'll be 500ms (Basti blocks). A lot is happening on DOT this year!
Also if you look at the stats the network has been growing over time https://data.parity.io/parachain-stats?selection-mode=include&frequency=daily

62080949
>>62079649
Also combine that with the new tokenomics and the long term vision for the project... I really don't see this project becoming irrelevant to be honest. It simply JUST FUCKING DOES WHAT IT'S SUPPOSED TO DO. The other L2s are behind and they won't catch up ever in my opinion. Once JAM hits DOT will be AT LEAST 5 to 10 years ahead of all the major L2s and L1s. It's not even comparable to be honest. People are blind to not see it. Sooner or later people will wake up and realize "OMG, I've been a degenerate memecoin gambler while I could buy the Vishnu of the blockchain".
Simply put, this project does what others promise and don't deliver. DOT has already DELIVERED and is DELIVERING even more amazing things that no other chain has and if they do they don't package it like DOT does. Sure ICP has continous compute, but does it have native interoperability and can host L2s and smart contracts and has 500ms block times and is decentralized and has shared security to his L2s and has the best governance system in crypto and has over a few million TPS and has ... and has ... Bro, it's fucking impossible to beat DOT on technical superiority. Some day people will realize that they can't build a new use case on the other chains. That they're LIMITING THEMSELVES by staying on shit like ARB or POL or some other fucking shit like that. A few months back Polkadot Asset Hub launched and people can simply copy paste EVM code to DOT and it'll work. We're currently waiting for this referendum (https://polkadot.polkassembly.io/referenda/1864) to pass in order for Uniswap and Safe to launch on Asset Hub. Other major projects will probably follow. Should pass any day now. Slow and steady wins the race

item.title: Advice on navigating a summer internship in a cast? — 28 replies — 112.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Advice on navigating a summer internship in a cast?

Open thread • Replies: 28 • Updated: 2026-04-08 22:01 UTC

OP

I just learned that my wrist is completely fucked and I need serious surgery to fix it. It’s likely kind of urgent which would likely force me to get surgery right a couple weeks before my internship starts in June. From there, I’ll likely spend 8-12 weeks in a cast, which is probably going to be my entire internship (and summer fml). I really don’t want to screw this opportunity up because it’s an MBA internship that could land me a full time role making quite a bit of money. Any advice?

Early replies (first 3)

62092016
My advice is for you to lose weight.

You can probably still type with a cast

62092037
>>62092016
I just worry that my work output is gonna be much slower than my peers who are all competing for a handful of slots. I definitely should be able to, but I worry the timing is really bad. Not much I can do about it though. The brace I’m in now is just until surgery actually gets done. The cast will be much more substantial when it’s put on

62092119
>>62091965
What’s the internship?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62092131
>>62092119
It’s a commercial leadership development internship program for a pharmaceutical company. Basically if I do well, I can get a full time offer for the actual LDP.

62092144
Is that a scaphoid fracture I see?

62092148
>>62092131
Congrats!
Will you be doing a lot of excel / PPT work? That’s the only way I see your hand being an issue, but even then it’s surmountable

62092162
>>62092037
I have a programmer friend who only has two fingers on his right hand. The quality of your work won’t depend on a cast.

62092164
>>62092144
Unfortunately not. It’s a scapholunate rupture and a TFCC. I wasn’t lying about my wrist being destroyed lol. The surgeon I saw yesterday described my SL ligament as being “gone” haha fml

62092173
>>62092148
Thanks! Yeah, it’s probably gonna be a lot of PowerPoint and emails. Less so Excel, but it’s certainly possible I’ll be doing a lot of that too

62092182
>>62092162
Fair, it’s more the timeliness of things is all. I’m happy to work late to get things done if that’s what it takes, but I just would hate if this already crappy situation is made worse by costing me a great opportunity.

62092342
>>62092173
Relax, no one types anymore, it's all voice input now. I haven't done it in half a year or so outside 4chan, and I do real estate and SaaS for a living.
>commercial leadership development internship program for a pharmaceutical company
You are either in the top 20% height and ick-free or rich in blackmail to land you a 100% qualification-free representative leech role, or you become self employed ASAP. The default hypothesis is that AI is better than humans in management in one or two years, which means the rich run all business ventures without entrepreneurs through some SaaS that gives them a pile of paper to sign every week while wrangling a flock of minimum wage slaves, essentially AI's biobots. It will have a nice layer of comfortable lies on top, they will call the poverty slaves turbo empowered AI leveraging entrepreneurs or something, but that will be the flow of wealth and control, AI abolishes capital-less entrepreneurship. That means you need to start a venture where that can not be done, or while it can not be done yet, and work 100 hours a week to escape the pods and drafts.

62092485
>>62091965
>>62092037
bro. I understand you want an actual answer here but listen to the other guy. your main problem here is not the internship or your broken hand its your body weight. lose weight NOW, this is more important than a retarded internship. you are fucking fat.

62092556
>>62092485
Brother, respectfully, I’m literally a Marine. You got one look at my arm in a brace. I’m 6’2” 215. I’m not skinny, but I really don’t think I’m fat lol

62092657
>>62091965
If you live in the US, the ADA should protect you. Stop being a faggot and do 3 seconds of research next time.

If not, why are you even pursuing an MBA? Go study sheep shearing or whatever the hell you Europeans do.

62092687
>>62092657

62093544

Also, just to prove to y’all I’m not lying (this bothered me, not sure why) I’m not fat, I promise. I could probably drop a few, but no more that like 5-10 lbs

As far as changing the cast out, it sounds like it’ll be only 1-2 casts during the whole internship, so not a huge opportunity to change it out.

>>62093975
The fingers I can’t change. They’ve always been that way, even when I was ripped as seen here lol. Admittedly it’s been a few years and a few pounds since then, but “fat as shit” I’d wild. Overweight, maybe. Not borderline clinically obese lol

Typing with one hand and occasionally using the fingers on the other one. I got faster within days, the boss and coworkers were sympathetic so it didn't worsen my standing at the office.

ok bro, anyways it doesnt matter, ripped or not. women will fuck either way

and search up cast, wrap . or atheltic tape you can swap out over the cast , or even cast covers.

the idea is just to let other people know you are maintaining the cast thus you care about your wellbeing.

so if they see you taking care of yourself they will actually respect you and also want to take care of you...

this actually low key might help you in being more likeable. without them even really realizing it.

how cute would it be when some notice you swap out and have a nice clean cast all the tine

its like... youre so respeonsible.

use this to yor advagntage .

as long as you don't bring it up every conversation nobody will care after like the first 2 weeks. it also shows you have a somewhat active lifestyle as you likely hurt it playing a sport or working out.

>>62094119

do you have a food processor anon?
450g dry chickpeas in filtered water and a shake of baking soda overnight
rinse and into pot with enough filtered water to covers about a couple inches. throw in about 6 large nonchinese garlic cloves cut once or twice. pepper salt and a few bay leaves

whole bowl of fresh hummus that lasts awhile and is pretty healthy to fill you up for snacks. very easy process and cheap

OP

Should I get some cyber security certs? which ones should I focus on if yes?

Early replies (first 3)

62084718
Funny of you to ask OP, I remember my struggles of landing my first WFH job. Btw, WFH stands for Work from Home because not many people come across that term usually. But, if you must ask, our company is headquartered in Nashville. My base pay is 95k per year along with a COL stipend and generous health and pension benefits. As we speak, I am clocked into my position in the beautiful hot springs in British Columbia. We do have an RTO policy, but I haven't been to the office in 4 years! Ha-Ha! You COULDN'T pay me to work in the office. Yesterday I had fun fucking a uni student that transferred from china for the school years, so yeah life is great so far. Today she is making me her traditional Chinese dinner as a thank you for fucking her so nicely. Maybe next month I'll fly out to Holland and do my job for the month. But like I said OP, keep your chin up and don't be disuaded by the multiple rejection emails in your future until you get that golden offer. Take it and run with it. Anyways .... Chiao!

62084729
>>62084718

I went to meetings with the big dogs while my manager who knew less about our programs stayed with the rest of the team. I loved my bosses. They loved me. I got laid off after training 10 Filipinos. So did the rest of our NA Fraud team and our manager. Replaced by the Flips. I job hunted with good references and great resume. I only got 2 interviews in 6 months, finally landed a shitty call center job. Had to take it because I needed work.

No one is hiring WFH unless you're something special or have certs others cant easily get. Why pay you 20+/hr when they can hire a flip for 2.50/hr. You can do more work? Okay, I can hire 4 flips and its still less than hiring you. If not flips, indians.

62084650
>>62075553
It creats demand for dollars around the world. That keeps the price of dollars higher than it otherwise would be since new supply of dollars can be sent to other countries in exchange for manufacturered goods. This means the US doesn't need to have a massive manufacturing base at home which means less pollution and in general high stress and health risk jobs. Instead the US economy can function by basically finding various mechanisms to just hand the US population money which they can spend and eventually the money finds its way abroad. This creates a large service economy where most people don't actually do any "real work" like making shit. Now their job is just a way to inject money into the domestic economy so that it can then be sent over seas and then those people can use that money to buy things like US bonds(both corporate and government), oil, US stocks and other things that are traded in dollars. Jobs end up being less stressful, you work fewer hours, and there's lots of room to climb up since career ladders are just social structures to hand out money rather than actually give you real responsibility. There's still plenty of real jobs of course, but an insane amount of US jobs are the kind where you just sit at a computer and read emails and then send another email to someone else and that person tells the floormanager of a factory to tell their immigrant labor that there's overtime this weekend.

So Americans enjoy a high standard of living even compared to other developed countries. People own cars, get cheap fuel, imported goods are cheap, there's options to save with high returns and so on. If the dollar stopped being the reserve currency all the dollars that's currently floating around in the world economy would have little use outside the US and they'd flow back into the domestic economy. The dollar loses most of its value and the standard of living of Americans drops since they're losing access to foreign goods.
